name: Julia Tatum
focus: UAV and LiDAR remote sensing • Disturbance monitoring • Environmental resilience
bio: Julia Tatum is a researcher at Northern Arizona University whose work focuses on remote sensing, environmental monitoring, and ecosystem dynamics. Her research applies advanced technologies such as UAVs (drones) and LiDAR to study natural disturbances, including wildfires, flooding, and land-use change in wildland–urban interface environments. She integrates geospatial analysis, high-resolution imagery, and field data to better understand how ecosystems respond to environmental stressors and climate-related events. Tatum’s work contributes to improving methods for assessing landscape change and supports efforts in ecological management, hazard assessment, and environmental resilience.
